purpose: Compact UI/UX decision questions that drive creative variants and implementation choices.

questions.text: What should the product feel like: premium, friendly, technical, formal, playful, clinical, editorial, or operational?
Who is the primary user: customer, admin, operator, manager, executive, editor, clinician, field worker, or developer?
What is the main workflow on this screen: discover, compare, buy, approve, monitor, edit, search, report, onboard, or resolve?
Should the UI be compact, balanced, or comfortable?
How much trust/risk/compliance should the UI communicate: low, medium, high, finance, healthcare, security, or government?
Will the UI be English-only, Arabic-only, bilingual, or RTL-first?
Is there a preferred visual reference or should Kabeeri select a variant from the design system?
Should motion be minimal, balanced, or expressive?
Should navigation feel like sidebar, top navigation, tabs, split navigation, bottom mobile nav, or command center?
Should UI copy be concise, warm, formal, technical, reassuring, conversion-focused, or editorial?
